design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a Gōketsuji clan member and battle through sequential opponents to prove worthiness as clan leader, with transformations and movement mechanics creating varied combat approaches
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat execution against diverse opponent styles; clan succession tournament framing provides narrative stakes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a unique fighter from an absurdist martial arts family with comedic character archetypes and special transformation abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Series is explicitly known for absurdist humor and unconventional fighting game features: character transformations via throws, universal double-jump and run mechanics, and comedic character designs (strongwoman, playboy, martial arts family members). Rejects grittier fighting game conventions in favor of comedic tone

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Genesis version allows players to adjust special attack strength for each character, providing customizable difficulty tuning

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character selection determines fight progression; Oume Gōketsuji serves as final unplayable boss character
replayability_hooks: Eight selectable characters with distinct fighting styles and win quotes
Character transformations change appearance and fighting style via throwing moves
Genesis-exclusive Battle Royal mode with team selection and hidden character reveal
Multiple game modes including Vs. Battle, Practice, Time Attack, and Life Attack (defeat maximum opponents on single life bar)

world_building_techniques: Character-specific win quotes and endings reveal personality and motivation
Tournament framing: clan succession competition provides narrative context
Character archetypes with detailed backgrounds (Angela the Italian strongwoman, Annie the spoiled British heiress, Keith the playboy, Otane the abused sister training for revenge)

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss Oume Gōketsuji is palette-swap of selectable character Otane but serves as ultimate tournament goal; boss represents tournament prize (clan leadership) rather than separate combat archetype
notable_bosses: Oume Gōketsuji - 78-year-old clan matriarch, unplayable boss, superhuman fighter with mysterious attacks

faction_indicators: Gōketsuji clan membership (multiple playable characters from same family)
International tournament participants (Italian, English, American fighters competing)
Family power dynamics (Otane vs. Oume sibling rivalry central to narrative)
danger_signals: Oume's supernatural strength and mysterious attacks position her as antagonistic force
Tournament structure implies zero-sum competition for leadership position
environmental_storytelling: Character backgrounds and relationships implied through narrative endings
Clan legacy and family dysfunction revealed through character dialogue and endings
